result:
Existing tests still pass, and no `frontend/` or existing trip/checklist/auth files were touched — only new additive files. `docs/step2-extension/03-build.md` and `prompts.txt` were already modified before I started (pre-existing working-tree state), untouched by me.

## Summary

Implemented the weather forecast feature in `backend/`, following `docs/step2-extension/02-design.md` exactly:

- **`WeatherService`** — geocodes the destination, calls the forecast API, and looks up the trip's `startDate` in `daily.time`. All three degradation cases (zero geocoding results, date beyond the 16-day array, forecast API failure) collapse into `WeatherResponse.unavailable()` — nothing propagates as a 5xx.
- **`WeatherController`** — new `GET /api/trips/current/weather`, reusing `CurrentUserProvider`/`TripService` so it gets the same 401 (unauthenticated) and 404 (no active trip) behavior as existing trip endpoints, without touching `TripController`.
- **`WeatherResponse`** — `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` record matching the contract's exact shapes (`{"available": false}` vs. the full forecast object).
- **`WeatherClientConfig`** — a dedicated `RestTemplate` bean with 5s connect/read timeouts for both external calls.
- **`WeatherControllerTest`** — 6 tests using `MockRestServiceServer` (no live network calls): the 4 required scenarios from the Test Plan, plus 401/404 auth coverage for completeness.

`./gradlew build test` passes (13 tests total, 0 failures). Frontend and existing trip/checklist/auth code were not modified.

Implementation complete. Summary:

- **`frontend/src/api/client.js`** — added `getCurrentTripWeather()` calling `GET /api/trips/current/weather` (reuses the existing `request` helper, so `credentials: 'include'` is automatic).
- **`frontend/src/utils/weatherCodes.js`** — WMO code → human description map (clear/mostly clear, cloudy, fog, drizzle, rain light/moderate/heavy, freezing rain, snow, thunderstorm) with an "Unknown conditions" fallback.
- **`frontend/src/components/WeatherCard.jsx`** — fetches independently on mount via its own `useEffect`/state, never touching the trip fetch. Renders forecast on `available: true`, "Forecast available closer to your trip" on `available: false`, and "Weather unavailable right now" on any fetch failure (network error, non-200, or unexpected shape) — the second isolation layer per NFR3.
- **`frontend/src/components/TripDetailScreen.jsx`** — added `<WeatherCard destination={trip.destination} startDate={trip.startDate} />` between `Countdown` and `Checklist`, matching the updated component breakdown.
- **Tests**: `WeatherCard.test.jsx` covers available/unavailable/fetch-failure/non-200 cases; `TripDetailScreen.test.jsx` gained an isolation test proving RouteMap, Countdown, and Checklist still render when the weather fetch rejects outright.

`npm install`, `npm test -- --run` (10/10 passing), and `npm run build` all succeed. No backend/ or other component files (SearchScreen, auth, RouteMap, Countdown, Checklist) were touched.
